tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":57894,"title":"[Translation] Docs: Update CHANGELOG and README after generator migration","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.Translation.Document` **Service directory:** `sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/` **Triggered by:** PR #56970 (commit 085c6ca) by `@JonathanCrd` ## What Changed PR #56970 migrated `Azure.AI.Translation.Document` to the new Azure Data Plane SDK generator. The changes include: - Migrated codegen to use the new TypeSpec-based generator (updated `tsp-location.yaml` and project file) - Renamed `DocumentTranslationModelFactory` internal structure (Model Factory rename) - Custom code for `StartTranslation` removed — now fully generated - Credential logic in the custom layer updated after regeneration - `Translate` and `TranslateAsync` methods made public in the custom layer - `ForwardsClientCalls` attribute removed from client methods - `ExperimentalAttribute` added to project compilation sources - Diagnostic scopes fixed in Pageable methods ## Gaps Found - **CHANGELOG `2.1.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` is completely empty** — all subsections ("Features Added", "Breaking Changes", "Bugs Fixed", "Other Changes") are blank despite significant changes landing in this version - **README does not mention `ExperimentalAttribute`** — the library now marks itself as experimental but there is no note for users in the README about this status or what it implies - **README "Authenticate the client" section still only shows `AzureKeyCredential` and `DefaultAzureCredential`** — the updated credential logic in the custom layer (post-regeneration) is not reflected with a note about any behavioral changes - **No mention of `SingleDocumentTranslationClient.Translate` / `TranslateAsync` visibility change** — these methods are now public (custom layer) but this change is not surfaced in docs or changelog 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/CHANGELOG.md` Edit the `## 2.1.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` section. Replace the empty body with entries reflecting the actual changes from PR #56970: ```markdown ## 2.1.0-beta.1 (Unreleased) ### Features Added - `SingleDocumentTranslationClient.Translate` and `SingleDocumentTranslationClient.TranslateAsync` are now publicly accessible. ### Breaking Changes - The library is now generated using the new Azure TypeSpec-based SDK generator. Some internal implementation details have changed, though the public API surface remains largely compatible with 2.0.0. - `ForwardsClientCalls` attribute has been removed from client methods. ### Other Changes - Migrated code generation to the new TypeSpec-based Azure SDK generator for improved maintainability. - Updated credential handling logic in the client custom layer. - `ExperimentalAttribute` has been added to the package; this library is under active development and the API surface may change. ``` #### File 2: `sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/README.md` Add a note at the top of the **Getting started** section (right after the introductory paragraph and the badge links, before the `## Getting started` heading) to inform users of the experimental status: ```markdown > **Note:** This library is currently in an experimental state. The API surface may change in future releases. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new snippet test files need to be created for these changes — the existing samples in `sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/tests/Samples/` cover the main usage patterns. However, if the agent wants to add a snippet showing `SingleDocumentTranslationClient.Translate` usage, follow this structure: 1. **Write sample code** in `sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/tests/Samples/Sample6_SingleDocumentTranslation.cs` (check if it already exists; if so, update it): - Wrap the `Translate` method example with `#region Snippet:SingleDocumentTranslation` / `#endregion` - Add `[Test]` attribute 2. **Add placeholder in README** in the `## Examples` section under a new heading `### Translate a single document`: ``` ``` ``` ``` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README currently contains all required sections. Verify each is complete: 1. ✅ Getting started (install, prerequisites, authenticate) 2. ✅ Key concepts 3. ✅ Examples (async and sync) 4. ✅ Troubleshooting (via reference links) 5. ✅ Next steps 6. ✅ Contributing The main gap is the missing experimental status note and the incomplete CHANGELOG. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 translation` 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 translation` 5. `dotnet format sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24106046024/agentic_workflow) · ● 307.3K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57894","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-07T21:56:11Z","updated_at":"2026-04-07T21:56:11Z"}

However, if the agent wants to add a snippet showing `SingleDocumentTranslationClient.Translate` usage, follow this structure: 1. **Write sample code** in `sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/tests/Samples/Sample6_SingleDocumentTranslation.cs` (check if it already exists; if so, update it): - Wrap the `Translate` method example with `#region Snippet:SingleDocumentTranslation` / `#endregion` - Add `[Test]` attribute 2. **Add placeholder in README** in the `## Examples` section under a new heading `### Translate a single document`: ``` ``` ``` ``` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README currently contains all required sections. Verify each is complete: 1. ✅ Getting started (install, prerequisites, authenticate) 2. ✅ Key concepts 3. ✅ Examples (async and sync) 4. ✅ Troubleshooting (via reference links) 5. ✅ Next steps 6. ✅ Contributing The main gap is the missing experimental status note and the incomplete CHANGELOG. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 translation` 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 translation` 5. `dotnet format sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24106046024/agentic_workflow) · ● 307.3K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57894","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-07T21:56:11Z","updated_at":"2026-04-07T21:56:11Z"}

arguments.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This issue reports documentation gaps in `Azure.AI.Translation.Document` following a generator migration in PR #56970 — specifically an empty CHANGELOG section and missing README notes about experimental status and API visibility changes.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.Translation.Document` (v2.1.0-beta.1, unreleased)
**Affected API:** `SingleDocumentTranslationClient.Translate` / `TranslateAsync`, `DocumentTranslationModelFactory`, client credential layer
**Scenarios:**
- CHANGELOG `2.1.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` section is completely empty despite significant changes from the TypeSpec generator migration
- README does not mention the new `ExperimentalAttribute` added to the package
- README "Authenticate the client" section does not reflect post-regeneration credential logic changes
- `SingleDocumentTranslationClient.Translate`/`TranslateAsync` visibility change to `public` is not documented

**Root ask:** Update CHANGELOG and README to accurately reflect the changes introduced in PR #56970 (generator migration).
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only issue with no runtime bug. The gap is between the actual code changes (PR #56970) and the user-facing documentation artifacts.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review PR #56970 to confirm the full scope of changes (generator migration, method visibility, credential changes, `ExperimentalAttribute`)
2. Update `sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/CHANGELOG.md` — populate the `2.1.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` section with accurate "Features Added", "Breaking Changes", and "Other Changes" entries
3. Update `sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/README.md` to add an experimental status note and reflect any credential or API surface changes relevant to users
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — the issue concerns a non-management client SDK package (`Azure.AI.Translation.Document`) and its documentation artifacts
- **Service:** `Cognitive - Translator` — the package lives under `sdk/translation/` and maps to the Cognitive - Translator service label per CODEOWNERS
- **Confidence:** High — the package name and service directory are explicitly stated; there is no ambiguity between services or categories; the CODEOWNERS mapping is direct
</details>
